calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Keith Baraka For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; & Declaration Of Steven L. Robinson
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. STEVEN L. ROBINSON, Esq. of Law Offices of Joseph L. Alioto and Angela Alioto (Tel: 415-434-8700) for petitioner;
Atty. ROBERT ROGOYSKI, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-4267) for respondent City and County of San Francisco (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: PETITIONER KEITH BARAKA's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear to address the apparent confusion between CPF-22-517656 and CGC-20-587897.

Matter discussed.

The Court determines that though the moving party's motion papers reflected the correct case no. CGC-20-587897, the moving party inadvertently submitted their motion papers for efiling under the incorrect case no. CPF-22-517656 (which resulted in case no. CPF-22-517656 being calendared for today's hearing instead of case no. CGC-20-587897).

The Court continues the hearing on this motion to February 6, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion to be calendared under the correct case no. CGC-20-587897.

The parties are instructed to file/re-file all their moving papers, opposition papers, and reply papers under the correct case no. CGC-20-587897.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
Off Calendar May-23-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Stephen Robinson, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF JOSEPH L. ALIOTO AND ANGELA ALIOTO, (415) 434-8700, srobinson@aliotolawoffice.com for Petitioner Keith Barka, via Zoom Conference
Jennifer S. Stoughton, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-4267, jennifer.stoughton@sfcityatty.org, for Respondent CCSF (In Person)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KEITH BARAKA MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE

Petitioner's petition for writ of mandate is denied. (SEEWORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the Court's ruling verbatim in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER KEITH BARAKA'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
